<system-reminder>
The user named this session "beta-Bb-opulent-try-acg-scrub". This may indicate the session's focus or intent.
</system-reminder>

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  Second ACG audit pilot — prose-judgment focus;
  runs against known-positive specimens.
  
  ## Gate
  Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating), worked in the operator's chat —
  never mount or delegate this pace at a lesser tier.
  The pilot's primary deliverable is ACG amendment judgment —
  noticing where the guide is vague, wrong, or silent —
  and delegation makes that feedback secondhand.
  If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.
  
  ## Goal
  Audit the access-probe pair —
  RBGV (Tools/rbk/rbgv_probe.sh) against RBSAJ + RBSAO —
  under ACG's detect-only moves, with emphasis on ACGm_105 (paraphrase collapse)
  and ACGm_106 (ornament drain):
  the groom survey found spec and source wording the bounded
  propagation-absorbing-retry idea twice,
  and rationale-as-normative prose in RBSAJ.
  Twin deliverable: findings plus ACG amendments.
  
  ## Cinched
  - The known specimens serve as ACG rule-2 known answers:
    if the audit misses them, the detect rule is wrong —
    fix the guide, not the report.
  - Pilot-1 rulings are in ACG — apply, never re-derive:
    expect citation-collapse (ACGm_104's degenerate case) wherever a comment is
    already homed,
    judge edit-time Palisade content against the fallback bound,
    and flag any test names found in comments under the standing ruling.
    Aim amendment-hunting at ground pilot 1 did not cover —
    the prose moves are this pilot's territory.

    source-comment collapses whose residues would cite constraint anchors defer
    to the gated collapse pace;
    spec-side prose moves (one authoritative wording, quoin-ref citations
    elsewhere) need no anchor mechanism and may land as individually reviewed
    moves.
  - Triage rider: Manor/Payor/account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane —
    report, never touch.
  - Word-cancer findings cite the MCM antipattern home established by the
    preceding antipattern-catalog pace.
  
  ## Done when
  Findings memo with move-type tags,
  source-side deferrals routed to the gated collapse work;
  agreed spec-side collapses/drains landed as individually reviewed moves,
  one authoritative wording per idea with citations elsewhere;
  ACG amendments landed;
  fast suite green if any source file changed.

content.text: Let me read the paddock from the gazette before presenting.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb
2	
3	## Shape
4	
5	Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene grown into the ACG audit program, in two strata.
6	First, targeted allocation sweeps feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP
7	feature finalization — magic-string -> constant sweeps and noun-cluster
8	curation, each with a blast radius that merges trivially.
9	Second, the audit program: per-pair spec/module audits under the ACG move
10	catalog, run while ACG is young so the guide and the documents harden together.
11	The first pilot grew the program a third stratum:
12	mechanism and guide work the audits themselves surfaced —
13	the cited-constraint anchor design,
14	the jailer dialect guide,
15	and the deferred collapse landings they gate.
16	A seed, not a tree: scope stays tight to the source/spec allocation family.
17	Resist becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.
18	
19	## Governing idea
20	
21	The authority is ACG (Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md) —
22	the spine, the three homes, the move discipline, and the numbered move catalog
23	all live there.
24	This paddock cites and never restates
25	(ACG's statements -> definition-sites clause applied to itself;
26	an earlier revision of this paddock paraphrased the guide and has been drained).
27	
28	## Cinched
29	
30	- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
31	  authoring/allocation; asciidoc is MCM's domain).
32	  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG.
33	  Not released at MVP.
34	- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practice; no mandated universals;
35	  no qualify-time enforcement.
36	  Universals are candidates confirmed pace-by-pace.
37	- Audit pace shape: one pace = one spec-subdocument/bash-module pair,
38	  source-side and spec-side moves applied in one read;
39	  the inherently cross-document moves (paraphrase collapse, tidemark drain)
40	  get single corpus-wide sweep paces instead.
41	- Pilot posture: ACG is untested — three pilots run before any roster of pair
42	  paces is slated.
43	  Every pilot's deliverable is twofold: the findings,
44	  and the ACG amendments where the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent.
45	  Roster vs further pilots is a post-pilot groom decision.
46	- Pilot selection (groom survey): the sentry pair tests the edit-time/Palisade
47	  comment boundary;
48	  the access-probe pair carries known-positive paraphrase/ornament specimens,
49	  so the audit proves itself against a known answer;
50	  the conjure pair is the richest surface and the scale test,
51	  gated with the rest of the ₣BH lane.
52	- Pilots execute at top model tier in the operator's chat;
53	  delegation to lesser tiers is for the post-pilot roster only,
54	  after ACG hardens.
55	- Per-pace model tier rides in each docket's Character line
56	  (judgment/curation work at opus, the mechanical sweeps at sonnet);
57	  the pilot top-tier gate stays a hard Gate, not a Character hint.
58	- Antipattern catalog: mcm_antipattern broadens to cover delivery defects as
59	  well as document defects;
60	  Stun, Molehill, and word cancer all home in MCM's Antipatterns section,
61	  with ACG and the JJK gradient-delivery guidance citing rather than defining.
62	- Heat tipped toward guidance over code repair (operator ruling at pilot 1):
63	  the pilot verdict was a mature spec side and a double-written source side,
64	  so guide and mechanism quality is the multiplier;
65	  collapse/relocation landings defer behind the cited-constraint anchor
66	  mechanism so each residue lands once, citing an anchor.
67	  Pilot 1's rulings live in ACG, not here:
68	  the citation-collapse case on ACGm_104,
69	  the Palisade fallback bound in the three-homes section,
70	  the cited-constraint anchor deferred record with its two binding rulings,
71	  and the one-file-one-guide rule in Related Guides.
72	- Remaining pilots apply the citation-collapse pattern where it fits rather
73	  than re-deriving it; their amendment hunting aims at ground pilot 1 did not
74	  cover.
75	- The jailer dialect (zero-dependency in-vessel POSIX sh) gets its own guide,
76	  promoted ahead of rule-of-3;

78	  (Memos/memo-20260612-acg-pilot-sentry-findings.md).
79	- Motivating strategy (operator): regenerate-from-specs trials — cheaper
80	  models regenerating code from spec + guide, diffed against live code — as
81	  the future forcing function on guide/spec quality.
82	  Makes the dedup direction a prerequisite, not hygiene;
83	  shapes prioritization toward guides and mechanisms.
84	
85	## Sequencing & lanes
86	
87	Ungated front, in order: the access-probe pilot next,
88	then the cited-constraint anchor design,
89	then the jailer dialect guide,
90	then the sentry collapse landings the anchor mechanism gates.
91	The BUK test-vocab mint and the axvo symbol-lookahead amendment ride the back
92	of the ungated front:
93	the mint is self-contained in BUS0,
94	and the axvo pace is only partially gated —
95	its RBS0 migration share defers until ₣BH lands.
96	
97	The rest of the heat is gated on ₣BH:
98	
99	- Eviction (removing superseded terms) — ₣BH curates the KILL/KEEP list;
100	  the standing conformance engine's rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
101	  cutovers.
102	  ACG names that engine the canonical home for evicted-term assertions.
103	- The conjure pilot — its surface is under active ₣BH rewrite;
104	  findings against it now would go stale.
105	- The rbrv.env single-home sweeps and the noun-cluster curation —
106	  their files share ₣BH's rewrite surface.
107	- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
108	  eviction and never removes terms.
109	
110	Triage rider across all audit work:
111	Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane and
112	reliquary / Lode / Hallmark vocabulary is ₣BH's — report, never touch.
113	Verb clusters stay unslated until their owning heats land;
114	slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would re-create the drift
115	this heat exists to kill.
116	
117	## Done looks like
118	
119	ACG hardened through three audit pilots, amendments landed;
120	the antipattern catalog homed in MCM with citers repointed;
121	the cited-constraint anchor mechanism specified at its MCM/AXLA home;
122	the jailer dialect guide authored and registered;
123	the sentry collapses landed through the anchor mechanism and the pilot
124	findings memo retired;
125	the rbrv.env literal single-homed in bash and rust as the first worked
126	ACGm_101 application;
127	the noun clusters curated to single homes;
128	the post-pilot groom has decided roster vs further pilots.

131

134	runs against known-positive specimens.

148	and ACGm_106 (ornament drain):

# jjezs_paddock ₣Bb

## Shape

Pre-MVP source/spec hygiene grown into the ACG audit program, in two strata.
First, targeted allocation sweeps feathered in pace-at-a-time alongside MVP
feature finalization — magic-string -> constant sweeps and noun-cluster
curation, each with a blast radius that merges trivially.
Second, the audit program: per-pair spec/module audits under the ACG move
catalog, run while ACG is young so the guide and the documents harden together.
The first pilot grew the program a third stratum:
mechanism and guide work the audits themselves surfaced —
the cited-constraint anchor design,
the jailer dialect guide,
and the deferred collapse landings they gate.
A seed, not a tree: scope stays tight to the source/spec allocation family.
Resist becoming the junk drawer for unrelated cleanups.

## Governing idea

The authority is ACG (Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md) —
the spine, the three homes, the move discipline, and the numbered move catalog
all live there.
This paddock cites and never restates
(ACG's statements -> definition-sites clause applied to itself;
an earlier revision of this paddock paraphrased the guide and has been drained).

## Cinched

- ACG = "Allocation Coding Guide" (not "Asciidoc" — the guide governs source
  authoring/allocation; asciidoc is MCM's domain).
  Veiled/proprietary, guide-family sibling to BCG/RCG/WSG/CBG.
  Not released at MVP.
- v1 posture: bless and name emergent practice; no mandated universals;
  no qualify-time enforcement.
  Universals are candidates confirmed pace-by-pace.
- Audit pace shape: one pace = one spec-subdocument/bash-module pair,
  source-side and spec-side moves applied in one read;
  the inherently cross-document moves (paraphrase collapse, tidemark drain)
  get single corpus-wide sweep paces instead.
- Pilot posture: ACG is untested — three pilots run before any roster of pair
  paces is slated.
  Every pilot's deliverable is twofold: the findings,
  and the ACG amendments where the guide proved vague, wrong, or silent.
  Roster vs further pilots is a post-pilot groom decision.
- Pilot selection (groom survey): the sentry pair tests the edit-time/Palisade
  comment boundary;
  the access-probe pair carries known-positive paraphrase/ornament specimens,
  so the audit proves itself against a known answer;
  the conjure pair is the richest surface and the scale test,
  gated with the rest of the ₣BH lane.
- Pilots execute at top model tier in the operator's chat;
  delegation to lesser tiers is for the post-pilot roster only,
  after ACG hardens.
- Per-pace model tier rides in each docket's Character line
  (judgment/curation work at opus, the mechanical sweeps at sonnet);
  the pilot top-tier gate stays a hard Gate, not a Character hint.
- Antipattern catalog: mcm_antipattern broadens to cover delivery defects as
  well as document defects;
  Stun, Molehill, and word cancer all home in MCM's Antipatterns section,
  with ACG and the JJK gradient-delivery guidance citing rather than defining.
- Heat tipped toward guidance over code repair (operator ruling at pilot 1):
  the pilot verdict was a mature spec side and a double-written source side,
  so guide and mechanism quality is the multiplier;
  collapse/relocation landings defer behind the cited-constraint anchor
  mechanism so each residue lands once, citing an anchor.
  Pilot 1's rulings live in ACG, not here:
  the citation-collapse case on ACGm_104,
  the Palisade fallback bound in the three-homes section,
  the cited-constraint anchor deferred record with its two binding rulings,
  and the one-file-one-guide rule in Related Guides.
- Remaining pilots apply the citation-collapse pattern where it fits rather
  than re-deriving it; their amendment hunting aims at ground pilot 1 did not
  cover.
- The jailer dialect (zero-dependency in-vessel POSIX sh) gets its own guide,
  promoted ahead of rule-of-3;
  rationale recorded in the pilot findings memo
  (Memos/memo-20260612-acg-pilot-sentry-findings.md).
- Motivating strategy (operator): regenerate-from-specs trials — cheaper
  models regenerating code from spec + guide, diffed against live code — as
  the future forcing function on guide/spec quality.
  Makes the dedup direction a prerequisite, not hygiene;
  shapes prioritization toward guides and mechanisms.

## Sequencing & lanes

Ungated front, in order: the access-probe pilot next,
then the cited-constraint anchor design,
then the jailer dialect guide,
then the sentry collapse landings the anchor mechanism gates.
The BUK test-vocab mint and the axvo symbol-lookahead amendment ride the back
of the ungated front:
the mint is self-contained in BUS0,
and the axvo pace is only partially gated —
its RBS0 migration share defers until ₣BH lands.

The rest of the heat is gated on ₣BH:

- Eviction (removing superseded terms) — ₣BH curates the KILL/KEEP list;
  the standing conformance engine's rows populate per-cluster behind ₣BH's
  cutovers.
  ACG names that engine the canonical home for evicted-term assertions.
- The conjure pilot — its surface is under active ₣BH rewrite;
  findings against it now would go stale.
- The rbrv.env single-home sweeps and the noun-cluster curation —
  their files share ₣BH's rewrite surface.
- Cluster repair (curating living vocabulary to a single home) is disjoint from
  eviction and never removes terms.

Triage rider across all audit work:
Manor / Depot / account-role vocabulary is ₣BZ's lane and
reliquary / Lode / Hallmark vocabulary is ₣BH's — report, never touch.
Verb clusters stay unslated until their owning heats land;
slating against terms two hot heats are still moving would re-create the drift
this heat exists to kill.

## Done looks like

ACG hardened through three audit pilots, amendments landed;
the antipattern catalog homed in MCM with citers repointed;
the cited-constraint anchor mechanism specified at its MCM/AXLA home;
the jailer dialect guide authored and registered;
the sentry collapses landed through the anchor mechanism and the pilot
findings memo retired;
the rbrv.env literal single-homed in bash and rust as the first worked
ACGm_101 application;
the noun clusters curated to single homes;
the post-pilot groom has decided roster vs further pilots.

I'm Opus 4.8 (1M context) — `claude-opus-4-8[1m]`. Before I orient you on the work, this pace has a hard Gate I need to clear with you first, because it's the kind the gate itself says to stop and ask about.

**Pace goal (₢BbAAJ — pilot-audit-access-probes):** Run the second ACG audit pilot on the access-probe pair — RBGV (`rbgv_probe.sh`) against specs RBSAJ + RBSAO — hunting the *prose* moves (paraphrase collapse ACGm_105, ornament drain ACGm_106) against known-positive specimens, and deliver both the findings memo **and** the ACG amendments where the guide proves vague/wrong/silent.

**The Gate, and why I'm stopping:**

> Top-tier model only (Fable-class at slating)… If mounted on a lesser tier, stop and tell the operator instead of proceeding.

I'm Opus 4.8 (1M), not Fable 5. Fable 5 is listed as the current flagship in this environment, named separately from the Opus/Claude 4.X family. So I genuinely can't certify I'm "Fable-class" — I'm a top-tier *Claude 4.X* model, but not the named one. The pace's whole point is first-person ACG amendment judgment (noticing where the guide is silent), which is exactly what the gate is protecting, so I don't want to quietly decide this in my own favor.

Your call — three honest options:

- **Switch this chat to Fable 5** and re-mount (cleanest read of the gate).
- **Proceed on me (Opus 4.8 1M)** — if you judge that satisfies "top-tier" for this pilot. I'll do the work directly in-chat, no delegation, as the gate requires.
- **Hold** — you want to think about it.

Once tier is settled I'll surface the rest in layers: heat context, then the full docket, then a proposed approach. Which way do you want to go?

message.content: Unfortunately, Fable is not available.  Opus will you give this pace a try?  thanks for flagging this issue, but I want to give it a try now anywa

content.text:
Thank you — and noted: you've made the informed call with the gate flagged, which is exactly the resolution the gate was built for. Proceeding on Opus 4.8 (1M), in-chat, no delegation.

**Where this pace sits** (one breath of context, then I'll dig in):
- Pilot **2 of 3** in the ACG audit program. Heat ₣Bb is 4/15, racing.
- Pilot 1 (sentry pair) found everything double-written → citation-collapse; its rulings now **live in ACG**. My job is to *apply* them, not re-derive — and aim the amendment-hunting at ground pilot 1 didn't touch: the **prose moves** (ACGm_105 paraphrase collapse, ACGm_106 ornament drain).
- Two known-positive specimens are already on the table from the groom survey: the *bounded propagation-absorbing-retry* idea written twice (spec + source), and rationale-as-normative prose in RBSAJ. These are the rule-2 known answers — if my audit misses them, the detect rule is wrong, and I fix the guide.

Silks **fits** the docket; no rename.

**Approach:** opus, in-chat, sequential. First a read pass over the corpus — ACG (the authority), both specs, the source module, and pilot-1's findings memo for the pattern — then I conduct the audit and report tagged findings before drafting any amendment or move. Starting the read pass now.

1	<!--
2	Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	All rights reserved.
4	SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Proprietary
5	-->
6	
7	# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home
8	
9	## Purpose
10	
11	ACG codifies one discipline: **don't recreate inline what already has a named
12	home — reference the home.** It is the language-neutral, allocation-facing
13	sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
14	(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
15	*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
16	already exists: point at it, never restate it.
17	
18	ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
19	in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
20	than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
21	and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.
22	
23	ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
24	does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
25	candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
26	in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
27	not repair.
28	
29	## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog
30	
31	ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:
32	
33	- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
34	  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
35	  number.
36	- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,
37	  each of which something *points at*: a constant's breadcrumb comment, a
38	  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
39	  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.
40	
41	This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
42	discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.
43	
44	## The spine — reference the home
45	
46	The governing idea, stated once:
47	
48	> Don't recreate inline what has a named home. **Values → constants; concepts →
49	> quoin-refs; statements → definition sites.** MCM builds the homes; ACG governs
50	> source's duty to reference rather than recreate.
51	
52	This is the **allocation instance of Load-Bearing Complexity** (CLAUDE.md). A
53	recreated value or name is a non-load-bearing duplicate: its existence adds drift
54	risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap
55	between intent and behavior the principle forbids. BCG's and RCG's Constant
56	Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
57	ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
58	single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
59	goes.*
60	
61	## The three homes — allocate by when-read
62	
63	A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:
64	
65	| When read | Home | Carries |
66	|-----------|------|---------|
67	| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
68	| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
69	| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |
70	| **History-time** | the commit | when and why a thing changed — git log and blame own it; version narration in the document body is a skidmark (MCM `mcm_skidmark`) |
71	
72	Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
73	execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
74	of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
75	announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read
76	by no one with a reason to correct it when it drifts.
77	
78	**The edit-time Palisade license is a fallback home, not a co-equal one.**
79	Palisade characterization obeys the same single-authoritative-wording rule as
80	every other statement: when a spec home carries the characterization, the spec is
81	authoritative and the comment keeps only the **residue** — the one-line
82	signature, the do-not-"fix" tripwire, and the citation. The full characterization
83	lives at the code only where no spec home exists. The bound serves the context
84	economy: code is the most-frequently-loaded medium, so it carries what an editor
85	needs *before* deciding to load the spec; the spec carries everything needed
86	after. Worked ruling: the sentry-pair audit, where the spec side already carried
87	every detected comment's content.
88	
89	Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:
90	
91	- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
92	  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
93	- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
94	  is doing. Read when running it.
95	
96	Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
97	practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
98	announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
99	announcement of the contract, not contamination.
100	
101	**The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as
102	a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product
103	does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits
104	it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace**
105	(diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per
106	project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns
107	its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like
108	an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**
109	
110	## The third clause — statements → definition sites
111	
112	The spine governs prose, not only values and names. Every normative
113	idea — a rule, a scope claim, a constraint, a rationale — has exactly one
114	authoritative wording at one definition site. Every other appearance is a
115	**citation**: it may name the home and orient the reader; it may not restate
116	the content in other words.
117	
118	A paraphrase is a fork — a second wording that drifts independently, leaving a
119	reader unable to know which wording is law. Its metastatic form is the
120	**word cancer** (concept home: MCM `mcm_word_cancer`) — the most recurrent
121	failure mode of LLM-assisted maintenance of large codebases, and the defect
122	this clause exists to forbid. A quoin gives a concept one string; this clause
123	gives a claim one wording — the prose analogue of the prefix-collision
124	mechanism in `Memos/retired/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`.
125	Canonical specimen: the 2026-06-10 Diff-Friendly-Prose repair, where a context
126	file *characterized* a spec's scope instead of citing it, and the
127	characterization carried the error for months.
128	
129	**Normative register** rides this clause on the spec side. In a spec, every
130	sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently
131	because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt (MCM's task
132	lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
133	guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
134	implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
135	knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
136	above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.
137	
138	## The fourth clause — wire formats are named homes
139	
140	A JSON document this project authors on **both sides** — an envelope, a result
141	slot, a substitutions blob — is itself a home: a wire format with senders and
142	receivers that must stay findable from each other. Its keys are therefore
143	**minted names** (CLAUDE.md mint: any persistent name anywhere is in scope),
144	carrying one key-sprue per format: `rblv_digest`, never bare `digest`. The sprue
145	is what makes the format greppable — `grep rblv_` returns the author, every
146	consumer, and the schema definition, with no central registry to drift (the
147	`buo` tweak-sprue precedent; the `_RBGL_` substitution registers are the senior
148	worked example). One sprue per *format*, not per file or per domain: two formats
149	sharing a sprue blur the census that is the sprue's whole purpose.
150	
151	The boundary is the Palisade: JSON conforming to a **foreign** schema — a Cloud
152	Build envelope, an OCI manifest, a vendor API response — keeps the foreign keys.
153	Sprued keys inside a foreign document are the tell that someone is decorating a
154	schema they don't own.
155	
156	The clause is prospective: it binds every wire format as it is authored or
157	touched; no retroactive sweep is mandated. The conversion move is catalogued as
158	ACGm_108.
159	
160	## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules
161	
162	Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:
163	
164	> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
165	> is detect-and-report, never repair.**
166	
167	Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
168	plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
169	detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.
170	
171	Three rules ride that litmus:
172	
173	1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
174	   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
175	   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
176	   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
177	   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
178	2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
179	   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
180	   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
181	   recreated.
182	3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
183	   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
184	   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
185	   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.
186	
187	## The named home — the conformance fixture
188	
189	Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
190	**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
191	(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
192	is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
193	gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.
194	
195	The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
196	`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
197	awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
198	identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
199	`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
200	proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
201	(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.
202	
203	**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
204	is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
205	by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
206	(move discipline, rule 1).
207	
208	## The Move Catalog
209	
210	Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
211	**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
212	**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
213	mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
214	detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
215	101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.
216	
217	### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant
218	
219	- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
220	  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
221	  should. Not human-facing prose.
222	- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
223	  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_constants.sh`). The constant is ground
224	  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
225	- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
226	  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
227	- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
228	  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
229	- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
230	  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
231	- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
232	  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
233	  Exclude that class before sweeping.
234	- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
235	  application of this move.
236	
237	### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity
238	
239	- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
240	  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
241	- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
242	  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
243	  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
244	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
245	  exists.
246	- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
247	  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
248	- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
249	- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
250	  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
251	  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
252	  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
253	  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
254	  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
255	  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
256	  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).
257	
258	### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead
259	
260	- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
261	  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
262	- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
263	  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
264	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
265	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
266	- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
267	  mutated.
268	
269	### 🔍 ACGm_104: conceptual-comment eviction
270	
271	- **Detect:** an edit-time comment carrying design-time knowledge — what a thing
272	  *is*, why it is shaped so, how it relates to other concepts — rather than
273	  operational mechanics or Palisade characterization.
274	- **Authority:** the three-homes table. The spec quoin is the design-time home;
275	  where no quoin exists, the move creates or extends one, then cites it; where
276	  the home already carries the content, the move degenerates to
277	  citation-collapse (below).
278	- **Licensing:** detect-only for the sweep — relocation mutates two documents
279	  (source and spec) and a wrong move is not cheaply caught, so each relocation
280	  lands as its own reviewed move, never batch-applied.
281	- **Citation-collapse (the common case):** when the receiving home already
282	  carries the comment's content, nothing relocates — declare the authoritative
283	  side (move discipline, rule 1; the spec, per the Palisade fallback bound) and
284	  the comment shrinks to residue: signature + tripwire + citation. The
285	  sentry-pair pilot found every one of its detected comments already homed;
286	  expect collapse, not relocation, wherever the spec side is mature.
287	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter plus spec link validation.
288	- **Done:** a report of misallocated comments, each tagged with its receiving
289	  quoin (existing, proposed, or already-carrying); relocations and collapses
290	  land one reviewed move at a time.
291	
292	### 🔍 ACGm_105: paraphrase collapse
293	
294	- **Detect:** the same normative idea worded differently in two or more places —
295	  across specs, context files, handbooks, or comments. The smell: a reader could
296	  ask "which of these is law?"
297	- **Authority:** the statements → definition sites clause; the concept home is
298	  MCM's `mcm_word_cancer` quoin. Triage declares one home authoritative; every
299	  other site becomes a citation.
300	- **Licensing:** detect-only — the collapse mutates prose in several documents
301	  at once and judgment selects the surviving wording; never batch-applied.
302	- **Verifier:** none mechanized; recurrence is caught by re-running the detect
303	  sweep against the declared home.
304	- **Done:** for each word cancer: one authoritative wording at one site,
305	  citations elsewhere, the variant wordings gone.
306	
307	### 🔍 ACGm_106: ornament drain
308	
309	- **Detect:** spec sentences in normative position that neither constrain nor
310	  carry marked rationale or gestalt — prose an implementer or validator could
311	  delete without acting differently.
312	- **Authority:** the normative-register rule (the statements clause, above).
313	- **Licensing:** detect-only — whether a sentence constrains is judgment;
314	  draining is per-document and reviewed.
315	- **Verifier:** none in v1.
316	- **Done:** the spec reads as constraint plus marked rationale; deleted ornament
317	  is gone, demoted ornament is marked.
318	
319	### 🔍 ACGm_107: skidmark drain
320	
321	- **Detect:** version commentary in a durable document — revision markers, dated
322	  change narration, "new in vX" annotations. Durable means spec, guide, code, or
323	  context file; a dated artifact (memo, scar, commit message) is exempt — history
324	  is its content.
325	- **Authority:** the history-time row of the three-homes table; the concept home
326	  is MCM's `mcm_skidmark` quoin. The commit is the home — git log and blame carry
327	  the change story with a forcing function the document body lacks.
328	- **Licensing:** detect-only as a sweep; an individual drain is mutate-now when
329	  the deleted text is pure narration (the content survives in git), reviewed when
330	  narration is entangled with normative statements.
331	- **Verifier:** none in v1; candidate for a cheap regex pass in the future linter
332	  (date-shaped tokens and version markers outside memo paths).
333	- **Done:** durable documents read in the present tense; history questions answer
334	  through git.
335	
336	### 🔧 ACGm_108: bare wire-key → minted sprue
337	
338	- **Detect:** an RB-authored JSON wire format (both sender and receiver ours)
339	  whose keys are bare terms (`name`, `digest`, `stamp`) rather than sprue-carried
340	  minted names.
341	- **Authority:** the fourth clause (wire formats are named homes). The sprue
342	  itself is governed by the mint discipline (CLAUDE.md): one sprue per format,
343	  terminal-exclusive, used for nothing else.
344	- **Licensing:** mutate-now when the format's author and every consumer convert
345	  in one move and a live verifier covers the path — a rename either resolves
346	  everywhere or the suite fails loud. Detect-only when the consumers cannot all
347	  convert together; for an RB-authored format that inability is itself a finding
348	  worth recording.
349	- **Verifier:** the consuming suite tier for the format (e.g. the capture
350	  fixtures for the Lode vouch envelope), plus the census check: `grep <sprue>_`
351	  returns exactly the format's sites and nothing else.
352	- **Done:** every key in the format carries the sprue; the format's schema
353	  identifier (if it has one) is bumped; the sprue grep is the format's complete
354	  sender/receiver census. First worked application: the Lode vouch envelope
355	  (`rblv_`, schema `rbld-vouch-2`). Second worked application: the Lode slot/result
356	  format (`rbls_`, keys `rbls_slot_1`/`rbls_stamp`/`rbls_vouch`).
357	- **Triage:** foreign-schema documents are excluded by the Palisade boundary
358	  before any sweep. Pre-MVP formats convert with no dual-dialect reading — there
359	  is no compatibility surface; standing artifacts are re-captured or discarded,
360	  never grandfathered.
361	
362	## The AXLA/MCM interface
363	
364	### The lookahead scanning rule
365	
366	When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
367	**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
368	terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
369	`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
370	distinct token type and does not shift that count.
371	
372	### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)
373	
374	ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
375	implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
376	lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
377	(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
378	`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
379	*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
380	mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
381	rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
382	dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.
383	
384	### The cited-constraint anchor (deferred)
385	
386	The sentry-pair pilot surfaced a third census mechanism beside the quoin and the
387	sprue: a **cited constraint** — a normative proposition (an invariant, a
388	deliberate deviation, a foreign-behavior signature) that the spec defines once
389	under a minted ID and that code comments and tests cite. One grep then returns
390	the definition, every defending code site, and every attacking test — with no
391	registry to drift. The mechanism is MCM/AXLA-level design (a new category, not a
392	quoin variant) and is deferred to its own pace; two rulings bind now:
393	
394	- **Test names never appear in source comments.** A comment naming its defending
395	  tests goes stale on every test rename with no forcing function. The linkage
396	  inverts: the test cites the constraint it defends.
397	- **Tripwire residues cite the constraint anchor once it exists.** Until then a
398	  residue names the spec section in prose — and relocation sweeps that would
399	  write prose pointers at scale wait for the mechanism rather than landing every
400	  citation twice.
401	
402	## Related Guides
403	
404	Which guide governs a file is itself an allocation question with a single
405	answer: **one file answers to exactly one guide, chosen by its environment.**
406	Guides may overlap in content; a file is never subject to two.
407	
408	- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
409	  bash instances of the spine.
410	- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
411	  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
412	- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
413	- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
414	- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
415	  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
416	- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
417	  themselves.
418	
419	## Acronym Registry
420	
421	| Term | Expansion |
422	|------|-----------|
423	| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
424	| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs, statements → definition sites |
425	| Wire format | An RB-authored JSON document with senders and receivers; a named home whose keys are minted (sprued) names — the fourth clause |
426	| Word cancer | Variations of one normative idea scattered as paraphrases, each drifting independently; the metastatic violation of statements → definition sites. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_word_cancer` |
427	| Normative register | Spec-side rule: every sentence constrains or is marked rationale/gestalt; unmarked ornament is contamination |
428	| Skidmark | Version commentary stranded in a durable document; history-time knowledge whose home is the commit. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_skidmark` |
429	| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
430	| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
431	| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
432	| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
433	| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |
434	| Citation-collapse | ACGm_104's degenerate case: the receiving home already carries the comment's content, so the comment shrinks to residue and nothing relocates |
435	| Residue | What survives at the code after a collapse: the one-line signature, the do-not-"fix" tripwire, and the citation |
436	| Cited constraint | Deferred third census mechanism beside quoin and sprue: a normative proposition the spec defines under a minted ID and that code and tests cite (see The AXLA/MCM interface) |
437

<!--

# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home

## Purpose

ACG codifies one discipline: **don't recreate inline what already has a named
home — reference the home.** It is the language-neutral, allocation-facing
sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
already exists: point at it, never restate it.

ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.

ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
not repair.

## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog

ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:

- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
  number.
- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,
  each of which something *points at*: a constant's breadcrumb comment, a
  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.

This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.

## The spine — reference the home

The governing idea, stated once:

> Don't recreate inline what has a named home. **Values → constants; concepts →
> quoin-refs; statements → definition sites.** MCM builds the homes; ACG governs
> source's duty to reference rather than recreate.

This is the **allocation instance of Load-Bearing Complexity** (CLAUDE.md). A
recreated value or name is a non-load-bearing duplicate: its existence adds drift
risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap
between intent and behavior the principle forbids. BCG's and RCG's Constant
Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
goes.*

## The three homes — allocate by when-read

A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:

| When read | Home | Carries |
|-----------|------|---------|
| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |
| **History-time** | the commit | when and why a thing changed — git log and blame own it; version narration in the document body is a skidmark (MCM `mcm_skidmark`) |

Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read
by no one with a reason to correct it when it drifts.

**The edit-time Palisade license is a fallback home, not a co-equal one.**
Palisade characterization obeys the same single-authoritative-wording rule as
every other statement: when a spec home carries the characterization, the spec is
authoritative and the comment keeps only the **residue** — the one-line
signature, the do-not-"fix" tripwire, and the citation. The full characterization
lives at the code only where no spec home exists. The bound serves the context
economy: code is the most-frequently-loaded medium, so it carries what an editor
needs *before* deciding to load the spec; the spec carries everything needed
after. Worked ruling: the sentry-pair audit, where the spec side already carried
every detected comment's content.

Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:

- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
  is doing. Read when running it.

Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
announcement of the contract, not contamination.

**The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as
a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product
does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits
it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace**
(diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per
project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns
its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like
an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**

## The third clause — statements → definition sites

The spine governs prose, not only values and names. Every normative
idea — a rule, a scope claim, a constraint, a rationale — has exactly one
authoritative wording at one definition site. Every other appearance is a
**citation**: it may name the home and orient the reader; it may not restate
the content in other words.

A paraphrase is a fork — a second wording that drifts independently, leaving a
reader unable to know which wording is law. Its metastatic form is the
**word cancer** (concept home: MCM `mcm_word_cancer`) — the most recurrent
failure mode of LLM-assisted maintenance of large codebases, and the defect
this clause exists to forbid. A quoin gives a concept one string; this clause
gives a claim one wording — the prose analogue of the prefix-collision
mechanism in `Memos/retired/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`.
Canonical specimen: the 2026-06-10 Diff-Friendly-Prose repair, where a context
file *characterized* a spec's scope instead of citing it, and the
characterization carried the error for months.

**Normative register** rides this clause on the spec side. In a spec, every
sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently
because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt (MCM's task
lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.

## The fourth clause — wire formats are named homes

A JSON document this project authors on **both sides** — an envelope, a result
slot, a substitutions blob — is itself a home: a wire format with senders and
receivers that must stay findable from each other. Its keys are therefore
**minted names** (CLAUDE.md mint: any persistent name anywhere is in scope),
carrying one key-sprue per format: `rblv_digest`, never bare `digest`. The sprue
is what makes the format greppable — `grep rblv_` returns the author, every
consumer, and the schema definition, with no central registry to drift (the
`buo` tweak-sprue precedent; the `_RBGL_` substitution registers are the senior
worked example). One sprue per *format*, not per file or per domain: two formats
sharing a sprue blur the census that is the sprue's whole purpose.

The boundary is the Palisade: JSON conforming to a **foreign** schema — a Cloud
Build envelope, an OCI manifest, a vendor API response — keeps the foreign keys.
Sprued keys inside a foreign document are the tell that someone is decorating a
schema they don't own.

The clause is prospective: it binds every wire format as it is authored or
touched; no retroactive sweep is mandated. The conversion move is catalogued as
ACGm_108.

## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules

Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:

> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
> is detect-and-report, never repair.**

Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.

Three rules ride that litmus:

1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
   recreated.
3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.

## The named home — the conformance fixture

Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.

The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.

**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
(move discipline, rule 1).

## The Move Catalog

Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.

### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant

- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
  should. Not human-facing prose.
- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_constants.sh`). The constant is ground
  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
  Exclude that class before sweeping.
- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
  application of this move.

### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity

- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
  exists.
- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).

### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead

- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
  mutated.

### 🔍 ACGm_104: conceptual-comment eviction

- **Detect:** an edit-time comment carrying design-time knowledge — what a thing
  *is*, why it is shaped so, how it relates to other concepts — rather than
  operational mechanics or Palisade characterization.
- **Authority:** the three-homes table. The spec quoin is the design-time home;
  where no quoin exists, the move creates or extends one, then cites it; where
  the home already carries the content, the move degenerates to
  citation-collapse (below).
- **Licensing:** detect-only for the sweep — relocation mutates two documents
  (source and spec) and a wrong move is not cheaply caught, so each relocation
  lands as its own reviewed move, never batch-applied.
- **Citation-collapse (the common case):** when the receiving home already
  carries the comment's content, nothing relocates — declare the authoritative
  side (move discipline, rule 1; the spec, per the Palisade fallback bound) and
  the comment shrinks to residue: signature + tripwire + citation. The
  sentry-pair pilot found every one of its detected comments already homed;
  expect collapse, not relocation, wherever the spec side is mature.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter plus spec link validation.
- **Done:** a report of misallocated comments, each tagged with its receiving
  quoin (existing, proposed, or already-carrying); relocations and collapses
  land one reviewed move at a time.

### 🔍 ACGm_105: paraphrase collapse

- **Detect:** the same normative idea worded differently in two or more places —
  across specs, context files, handbooks, or comments. The smell: a reader could
  ask "which of these is law?"
- **Authority:** the statements → definition sites clause; the concept home is
  MCM's `mcm_word_cancer` quoin. Triage declares one home authoritative; every
  other site becomes a citation.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — the collapse mutates prose in several documents
  at once and judgment selects the surviving wording; never batch-applied.
- **Verifier:** none mechanized; recurrence is caught by re-running the detect
  sweep against the declared home.
- **Done:** for each word cancer: one authoritative wording at one site,
  citations elsewhere, the variant wordings gone.

### 🔍 ACGm_106: ornament drain

- **Detect:** spec sentences in normative position that neither constrain nor
  carry marked rationale or gestalt — prose an implementer or validator could
  delete without acting differently.
- **Authority:** the normative-register rule (the statements clause, above).
- **Licensing:** detect-only — whether a sentence constrains is judgment;
  draining is per-document and reviewed.
- **Verifier:** none in v1.
- **Done:** the spec reads as constraint plus marked rationale; deleted ornament
  is gone, demoted ornament is marked.

### 🔍 ACGm_107: skidmark drain

- **Detect:** version commentary in a durable document — revision markers, dated
  change narration, "new in vX" annotations. Durable means spec, guide, code, or
  context file; a dated artifact (memo, scar, commit message) is exempt — history
  is its content.
- **Authority:** the history-time row of the three-homes table; the concept home
  is MCM's `mcm_skidmark` quoin. The commit is the home — git log and blame carry
  the change story with a forcing function the document body lacks.
- **Licensing:** detect-only as a sweep; an individual drain is mutate-now when
  the deleted text is pure narration (the content survives in git), reviewed when
  narration is entangled with normative statements.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; candidate for a cheap regex pass in the future linter
  (date-shaped tokens and version markers outside memo paths).
- **Done:** durable documents read in the present tense; history questions answer
  through git.

### 🔧 ACGm_108: bare wire-key → minted sprue

- **Detect:** an RB-authored JSON wire format (both sender and receiver ours)
  whose keys are bare terms (`name`, `digest`, `stamp`) rather than sprue-carried
  minted names.
- **Authority:** the fourth clause (wire formats are named homes). The sprue
  itself is governed by the mint discipline (CLAUDE.md): one sprue per format,
  terminal-exclusive, used for nothing else.
- **Licensing:** mutate-now when the format's author and every consumer convert
  in one move and a live verifier covers the path — a rename either resolves
  everywhere or the suite fails loud. Detect-only when the consumers cannot all
  convert together; for an RB-authored format that inability is itself a finding
  worth recording.
- **Verifier:** the consuming suite tier for the format (e.g. the capture
  fixtures for the Lode vouch envelope), plus the census check: `grep <sprue>_`
  returns exactly the format's sites and nothing else.
- **Done:** every key in the format carries the sprue; the format's schema
  identifier (if it has one) is bumped; the sprue grep is the format's complete
  sender/receiver census. First worked application: the Lode vouch envelope
  (`rblv_`, schema `rbld-vouch-2`). Second worked application: the Lode slot/result
  format (`rbls_`, keys `rbls_slot_1`/`rbls_stamp`/`rbls_vouch`).
- **Triage:** foreign-schema documents are excluded by the Palisade boundary
  before any sweep. Pre-MVP formats convert with no dual-dialect reading — there
  is no compatibility surface; standing artifacts are re-captured or discarded,
  never grandfathered.

## The AXLA/MCM interface

### The lookahead scanning rule

When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
distinct token type and does not shift that count.

### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)

ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.

### The cited-constraint anchor (deferred)

The sentry-pair pilot surfaced a third census mechanism beside the quoin and the
sprue: a **cited constraint** — a normative proposition (an invariant, a
deliberate deviation, a foreign-behavior signature) that the spec defines once
under a minted ID and that code comments and tests cite. One grep then returns
the definition, every defending code site, and every attacking test — with no
registry to drift. The mechanism is MCM/AXLA-level design (a new category, not a
quoin variant) and is deferred to its own pace; two rulings bind now:

- **Test names never appear in source comments.** A comment naming its defending
  tests goes stale on every test rename with no forcing function. The linkage
  inverts: the test cites the constraint it defends.
- **Tripwire residues cite the constraint anchor once it exists.** Until then a
  residue names the spec section in prose — and relocation sweeps that would
  write prose pointers at scale wait for the mechanism rather than landing every
  citation twice.

## Related Guides

Which guide governs a file is itself an allocation question with a single
answer: **one file answers to exactly one guide, chosen by its environment.**
Guides may overlap in content; a file is never subject to two.

- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
  bash instances of the spine.
- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
  themselves.

## Acronym Registry

| Term | Expansion |
|------|-----------|
| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs, statements → definition sites |
| Wire format | An RB-authored JSON document with senders and receivers; a named home whose keys are minted (sprued) names — the fourth clause |
| Word cancer | Variations of one normative idea scattered as paraphrases, each drifting independently; the metastatic violation of statements → definition sites. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_word_cancer` |
| Normative register | Spec-side rule: every sentence constrains or is marked rationale/gestalt; unmarked ornament is contamination |
| Skidmark | Version commentary stranded in a durable document; history-time knowledge whose home is the commit. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_skidmark` |
| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |
| Citation-collapse | ACGm_104's degenerate case: the receiving home already carries the comment's content, so the comment shrinks to residue and nothing relocates |
| Residue | What survives at the code after a collapse: the one-line signature, the do-not-"fix" tripwire, and the citation |
| Cited constraint | Deferred third census mechanism beside quoin and sprue: a normative proposition the spec defines under a minted ID and that code and tests cite (see The AXLA/MCM interface) |
